title: How to extract the username from an email address
task: Get the part before the @ — for display names, matching, or de-duplication.
explanation: FIND locates the @ and LEFT keeps everything before it: "ada.lovelace". The companion extraction — the domain after the @ — is covered in the extract-domain-from-email recipe; together they split any address in two. To go further and title-case a name out of the username, chain the sentence-case and substitute-dots-for-spaces patterns: =PROPER(SUBSTITUTE(LEFT(A2,FIND("@",A2)-1),"."," ")) gives "Ada Lovelace".
